A.text: It stays as a smooth, thick disk throughout.

B.text: It starts as a thin and rough disk and becomes a thicker, smoother disk.

C.text: It starts as a donut, becomes a disk, and then breaks apart into thin streams.

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from mpl_toolkits.mplot3d import Axes3D

y = np.linspace(-5, 5, 100)

# Create a 3D plot
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')

# Plot the surface
ax.plot_surface(x, y, z, cmap='viridis')

# Set labels
ax.set_xlabel('X axis')
ax.set_ylabel('Y axis')
ax.set_zlabel('Z axis')

# Show the plot
plt.show()
